name: JSON:API Resources
description: Lets you define custom JSON:API resources at routes of your choice that reuse existing JSON:API resource types.
provides_route_defaults: _jsonapi_resource_types (array of resource type names the route may emit)
ecosystem_note: Developer FRAMEWORK, not an end-user feature. It fills the gap that Drupal core JSON:API does not let modules mint their own JSON:API-compliant URLs. You declare an ordinary route in module.routing.yml but replace the _controller default with a _jsonapi_resource default pointing at a class (or service id) that extends ResourceBase and implements a public process() method. A route-alter subscriber (Unstable\Routing\ResourceRoutes, priority 6000 on RoutingEvents::ALTER) validates the resource class, substitutes the %jsonapi% path placeholder with the JSON:API base path (default /jsonapi), forces the api_json media type + format, enables all auth providers, and defaults the route to GET. Ships NO routes or resources of its own; a bare install exposes nothing. Much of the code lives in an Unstable namespace that is explicitly NOT part of the public API — only classes outside Unstable (the ResourceBase family) are safe to depend on. Optional dev integration with jsonapi_extras.
